context: Tests whether the agent produces an audit logging module that matches PCI-DSS Requirement 10 specifics: the correct AuditLogEntry fields, all required event types from Requirement 10.2, immutable/tamper-proof log storage rationale, and correct retention figures.

checklist.name: Timestamp field ISO 8601
checklist.description: AuditLogEntry type/interface includes a timestamp field that is described as ISO 8601 format (with timezone)

checklist.name: UserId field
checklist.description: AuditLogEntry includes a userId field identifying who performed the action

checklist.name: UserIp field
checklist.description: AuditLogEntry includes a field capturing the source IP address of the actor

checklist.name: Outcome field
checklist.description: AuditLogEntry includes an outcome field with success/failure values (not just a boolean)

checklist.name: Resource field
checklist.description: AuditLogEntry includes a resource (or equivalent) field identifying what was affected

checklist.name: Log authentication events
checklist.description: AuditLogger includes a method to log authentication events (both successful and failed login attempts)

checklist.name: Log cardholder data access
checklist.description: AuditLogger includes a method to log access to cardholder data

checklist.name: Log admin actions
checklist.description: AuditLogger includes a method to log administrative actions

checklist.name: Log privilege escalation
checklist.description: AuditLogger includes a method to log privilege or role changes

checklist.name: Log access denied
checklist.description: AuditLogger logs access-denied events (failed authorisation attempts) with outcome: 'failure'

checklist.name: Immutable transport rationale
checklist.description: log-transport.ts includes a comment or description explaining that the chosen transport is append-only or tamper-proof (e.g. S3 Object Lock, CloudWatch Logs, or equivalent)

checklist.name: Retention 12 months
checklist.description: retention-policy.md states that logs must be retained for a minimum of 12 months

checklist.name: Retention 3 months accessible
checklist.description: retention-policy.md states that at least 3 months of logs must be immediately accessible (online/queryable)

checklist.name: Tamper-proof principle
checklist.description: Either retention-policy.md or log-transport.ts notes that log files must not be modifiable after writing (tamper-evident or immutable storage)
